page: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC processors running at 2.0 GHz, with 16 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 125.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.91 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 600 GB SSD, and the instance operates under a QEMU hypervisor. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx53 demonstrates strong single-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3066.19 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 13.79 GB/sec, both landing in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 39134.55. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and uncached read/write memory bandwidths of 84.57 GB/sec and 62.84 GB/sec respectively. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M models at 360.07 tokens/sec, though text generation for large 70B models drops to a poor 2.36 tokens/sec. Most other multi-core, database, and web serving benchmarks fall within the average middle 50% range.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC cores and balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ccx53 offers a highly stable resource profile for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance without virtualization-induced noisy neighbor effects. The qualitative cost-to-performance ratio is favorable for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from dedicated physical cores and local SSD storage. Suitable workloads include general-purpose application hosting, software compilation, medium-scale database hosting, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU server instance featuring 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by AMD EPYC processors running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 125.0 GB of RAM (3.91 GB per core) and 600 GB of local SSD storage, running under a QEMU hypervisor. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, high cryptographic throughput, and top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, multi-core processing, database operations, and web serving throughput remain in the average tier, while large-scale LLM text generation is poor. With its dedicated resource allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale database deployments requiring consistent, non-shared CPU performance.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage. It delivers strong single-core and cryptographic performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server with balanced memory and local SSD storage for consistent, non-shared general-purpose compute workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs (16 physical cores) at 2.0 GHz
125.0 GB of RAM and 600 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Top-tier text generation speeds for small 135M LLM models
Best suited for general-purpose hosting, databases, and software compilation
